demographics.living_situation: lives with mother and family in a shared household

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My little girl's head is crawling with bugs and she's scratched herself raw until the back of her head is bleeding. She won't stop digging at it, she's beside herself, and none of us have slept a wink for two nights. (Mum calling on her behalf.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's got much worse over the last two days, she's scratched her scalp until it's bleeding and scabbing, and we're desperate for something to stop the itch so she can finally sleep.

symptom_narrative.onset: she started saying something was moving in her hair about five days ago, with a bit of mild itching at first
symptom_narrative.duration: about five days, but really bad for the last two days
symptom_narrative.character: relentless, maddening itch — she scratches constantly, and now there are raw scratches and some scabby crusty bits on her scalp and the back of her neck
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's been — she can't leave it alone and it's kept the whole house awake
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over her scalp, worst behind her ears and at the back of her neck
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, and much worse at night when she's trying to settle
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really seems to help the itch
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it's worse at night and when she gets hot and worked up
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she's exhausted, cranky and tearful from no sleep; I can see tiny white specks stuck to her hairs and I actually saw things crawling on her scalp this evening
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she's had a bit of an itchy head before but never anything like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: she hasn't slept properly for two nights, she's miserable, and I've kept her home

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young school-aged child; several family members share sleeping space in the house, and I'm worried the others will catch it too

objective_data_offered.value: lots of tiny white oval specks stuck firmly to the hair strands, mostly behind the ears and at the back of the neck; raw scratch marks and small sores on the scalp and back of the neck, a few with dried yellowish crusts
mother says she saw small insects crawling and moving on the scalp near the skin this evening
about 37.1 on the home thermometer — she feels a little warm but no real fever
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: mother reports the child is agitated, tearful and constantly digging her fingers into her scalp, and that nobody in the house has slept for two nights
several family members share sleeping arrangements in the home
